zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

title: Naive forbidden-token grep produces false positives on legitimate domain vocabulary
captured_by: claude-orchestrator while running RUN-TRANSFER-001 (anti-overfit transfer test)

evidence.incident: When the transfer-test ran the forbidden-token grep over runs/transfer-test-mini/, the token 'Household' produced a match on the string 'Head of Household' inside the federal income tax golden dataset. 'Head of Household' is IRC §2(b) tax filing status — legitimate domain vocabulary in the income-tax archetype, not wealth.com leakage.
evidence.naive_policy_outcome: If the policy were 'reject any match', this legitimate IRS terminology would have to be removed, breaking the test case.

root_cause: The forbidden-token grep used naive substring matching without word boundaries and without a per-archetype allowlist of overlapping legitimate vocabulary.

skill_system_changes_applied.change: Added explicit anti-slop trigger: 'Anti-overfit forbidden-token grep that uses naive substring matching without a per-archetype allowlist of legitimate domain vocabulary'.

New canonical protocol: Step-1..Step-5 classification rubric (product-code leakage / cross-reference / legitimate-allowlist); word-boundary regex; per-archetype allowlist with citations (IRC, regulatory).

regression_check: Future transfer tests must classify grep matches into the three categories per the protocol; raw-count rejection is an anti-pattern. The transfer-test-acceptance-record schema codifies the classification structure.
candidate_validator: df-meta-attractor/scripts/validate_anti_overfit_grep.py — planned follow-up that automates classification by loading the parent's forbidden-token list + the per-archetype allowlist and exiting non-zero only when category-3 count > 0.
